# EdgeOne Five-Minute ISR Stability Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Move all public blog content onto a unified five-minute SSG/ISR path on EdgeOne, preserve dynamic product features, and recover boundedly from transient Next.js page-data failures.

**Architecture:** Public HTML and `/_next/data` are generated by Next.js Pages Router SSG, refreshed by ISR every 300 seconds, and served stale when the platform can do so; all routes known at build time are pre-generated, while new paths retain blocking fallback. Narrow EdgeOne static rewrites repair the three locale-prefixed page-data shapes without middleware, and an isolated browser fetch wrapper retries only same-origin Next page-data GETs. API routes, comments, payments, subscriptions, authentication, and the knowledge graph remain outside public-page caching.

**Tech Stack:** Node.js 22, Next.js 14 Pages Router, React 18, Jest, pnpm 9.15.0, EdgeOne Makers, EdgeOne CLI 1.6.13, EdgeOne Cloud Functions.

## Global Constraints

- Work on `codex/stabilize-edgeone-isr` in the existing working directory; do not create a worktree.
- Preserve the user's uncommitted `.serena/project.yml` and `AGENTS.md`; never stage them.
- Use `pnpm`, never npm or yarn. Node.js must remain `>=20 <25`; deployment remains Node 22.
- Deployment remains `pnpm build`; do not change to `pnpm export`.
- The shared public-content revalidation value is exactly 300 seconds.
- Do not add a Notion webhook, a five-minute schedule, root `middleware.ts`, Clerk, or website-security acceleration.
- Do not cache `/api/*`, authentication, comments, payments, subscriptions, article-password responses, or mutation requests.
- Existing knowledge-graph refresh remains 10 minutes; only its Node Cloud Function maximum duration changes to 120 seconds.
- Page-data retry is limited to same-origin GET `/_next/data/*`, statuses 545/502/503/504 and network disconnects, with exactly two retries after 300 ms and 1000 ms.
- Never retry `AbortError`; never replay POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, or API calls.
- A hard-navigation recovery may occur at most once per Build ID and destination URL in a browser session.
- Production verification is read-only unless the user separately performs or authorizes a Notion edit, publish, unpublish, comment, or payment mutation.
- Every implementation task follows RED -> GREEN -> focused commit with Conventional Commit syntax.

---

## File Map

### New files

- `lib/cache/publicContentCache.js` — the one public-content ISR value resolver.
- `lib/client/nextDataRecovery.js` — scoped page-data retry, hard-navigation guard, and installer.
- `scripts/verify-edgeone-build-contract.js` — validates Next prerender manifests and EdgeOne locale-data rewrites.
- `scripts/probe-edgeone-stability.mjs` — canonical production warm/cold request probe and report writer.
- `__tests__/lib/cache/publicContentCache.test.js` — cache resolver unit tests.
- `__tests__/lib/client/nextDataRecovery.test.js` — page-data recovery unit tests.
- `__tests__/pages/public-isr-policy.test.js` — structural contract for all public SSG/ISR pages.
- `__tests__/scripts/verify-edgeone-build-contract.test.js` — manifest/rewrite verifier tests.
- `__tests__/scripts/probe-edgeone-stability.test.js` — probe parsing and result-summary tests.

### Modified files

- `blog.config.js` — change the default content revalidation from 60 to 300 seconds.
- `next.config.js` — emit a one-day `stale-while-revalidate` window for ISR responses.
- `lib/build/staticPaths.js` — generate every matching published path in a normal ISR build.
- `pages/index.js` — replace public SSR with SSG/ISR.
- `pages/archive/index.js` — replace public SSR with SSG/ISR.
- `pages/page/[page].js` — restore static paths and SSG/ISR pagination.
- `pages/[prefix]/index.js` — use the shared ISR helper.
- `pages/[prefix]/[slug]/index.js` — use the shared ISR helper.
- `pages/[prefix]/[slug]/[...suffix].js` — use the shared ISR helper.
- `pages/tag/index.js` — use the shared ISR helper.
- `pages/tag/[tag]/index.js` — use shared ISR and blocking fallback.
- `pages/tag/[tag]/page/[page].js` — use shared ISR and blocking fallback.
- `pages/category/index.js` — use the shared ISR helper.
- `pages/category/[category]/index.js` — use shared ISR and blocking fallback.
- `pages/category/[category]/page/[page].js` — use shared ISR and blocking fallback.
- `pages/search/index.js` — use the shared ISR helper.
- `pages/search/[keyword]/index.js` — use shared ISR and blocking fallback.
- `pages/search/[keyword]/page/[page].js` — use shared ISR and blocking fallback.
- `pages/_app.js` — install and clean up the page-data recovery wrapper.
- `edgeone.json` — add three narrow locale-data rewrites and set Node Cloud Function maximum duration to 120 seconds.
- `package.json` — add build-contract and production-probe scripts.
- `.gitignore` — keep local probe reports out of commits.
- `__tests__/lib/staticPaths.test.js` — replace the priority-only ISR expectation with all matching paths.
- `__tests__/pages/locale-routing.test.js` — replace the former SSR workaround contract with the locale SSG contract.
- `__tests__/pages/public-page-cache.test.js` — assert that private routes remain outside the public ISR policy.
- `docs/user-guide/config/site-basics.md` — document the 300-second default and request-triggered ISR semantics.
- `docs/user-guide/reference/features.md` — document the single public-content cache setting.
- `docs/user-guide/deploy/edgeone-pages.md` — document EdgeOne verification and rollback.

### Removed files

- `lib/cache/publicPageCache.js` — the old 60-second SSR-only header helper becomes unused.
- `__tests__/lib/cache/publicPageCache.test.js` — superseded by `publicContentCache.test.js`.

### Task 1: Establish One 300-Second Public ISR Policy

**Files:**
- Create: `lib/cache/publicContentCache.js`
- Create: `__tests__/lib/cache/publicContentCache.test.js`
- Modify: `blog.config.js:14`
- Modify: `next.config.js:436-440`

**Interfaces:**
- Consumes: `siteConfig(name, defaultValue, notionConfig)` from `lib/config.js`.
- Produces: `getPublicContentRevalidateSeconds(notionConfig): number | undefined` and `DEFAULT_PUBLIC_CONTENT_REVALIDATE_SECONDS = 300`.

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the failing resolver tests**

```js
jest.mock('@/lib/config', () => ({ siteConfig: jest.fn() }))

import { siteConfig } from '@/lib/config'
import {
  DEFAULT_PUBLIC_CONTENT_REVALIDATE_SECONDS,
  getPublicContentRevalidateSeconds
} from '@/lib/cache/publicContentCache'

describe('public content ISR policy', () => {
  const originalExport = process.env.EXPORT

  afterEach(() => {
    process.env.EXPORT = originalExport
    jest.clearAllMocks()
  })

  test('uses the approved 300 second value', () => {
    siteConfig.mockReturnValue(300)
    expect(DEFAULT_PUBLIC_CONTENT_REVALIDATE_SECONDS).toBe(300)
    expect(getPublicContentRevalidateSeconds({})).toBe(300)
  })

  test.each([undefined, null, 0, -1, NaN, 'invalid'])(
    'falls back to 300 for invalid value %p',
    value => {
      siteConfig.mockReturnValue(value)
      expect(getPublicContentRevalidateSeconds({})).toBe(300)
    }
  )

  test('accepts a positive numeric override', () => {
    siteConfig.mockReturnValue('600')
    expect(getPublicContentRevalidateSeconds({})).toBe(600)
  })

  test('disables ISR only for a real static export', () => {
    process.env.EXPORT = 'true'
    expect(getPublicContentRevalidateSeconds({})).toBeUndefined()
  })

  test('configures a one-day stale response window', () => {
    const fs = require('node:fs')
    const source = fs.readFileSync('next.config.js', 'utf8')
    expect(source).toMatch(/swrDelta:\s*86400/)
  })
})
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run the test and verify RED**

Run: `pnpm test -- __tests__/lib/cache/publicContentCache.test.js --runInBand`

Expected: FAIL with `Cannot find module '@/lib/cache/publicContentCache'`.

- [ ] **Step 3: Add the shared resolver and update the default**

Create `lib/cache/publicContentCache.js`:

```js
import BLOG from '@/blog.config'
import { siteConfig } from '@/lib/config'

export const DEFAULT_PUBLIC_CONTENT_REVALIDATE_SECONDS = 300

export function getPublicContentRevalidateSeconds(notionConfig) {
  if (process.env.EXPORT === 'true') return undefined

  const configured = Number(
    siteConfig(
      'NEXT_REVALIDATE_SECOND',
      BLOG.NEXT_REVALIDATE_SECOND,
      notionConfig
    )
  )

  if (!Number.isFinite(configured) || configured <= 0) {
    return DEFAULT_PUBLIC_CONTENT_REVALIDATE_SECONDS
  }

  return Math.floor(configured)
}
```

Change the `blog.config.js` entry to:

```js
NEXT_REVALIDATE_SECOND:
  process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_REVALIDATE_SECOND || 300, // Public content stays static for 5 minutes before the next request may trigger ISR regeneration.
```

Add the stale window to the existing `experimental` object in `next.config.js`:

```js
experimental: {
  swrDelta: 86400,
  scrollRestoration: true,
  optimizePackageImports: ['@heroicons/react', 'lodash']
}
```

With `revalidate: 300`, Next.js 14 uses this value to emit the equivalent shared-cache response policy `s-maxage=300, stale-while-revalidate=86400`. Do not add a broad `edgeone.json` cache rule.

- [ ] **Step 4: Run focused tests and verify GREEN**

Run: `pnpm test -- __tests__/lib/cache/publicContentCache.test.js --runInBand`

Expected: PASS, four test groups green.

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit only the policy files**

```bash
git add blog.config.js next.config.js lib/cache/publicContentCache.js __tests__/lib/cache/publicContentCache.test.js
git commit -m "feat(cache): unify public ISR at five minutes"
```

### Task 2: Pre-Generate Every Known Public Path and Keep Blocking Fallback

**Files:**
- Modify: `lib/build/staticPaths.js`
- Modify: `__tests__/lib/staticPaths.test.js`
- Create: `__tests__/pages/public-isr-policy.test.js`

- Modify: `pages/tag/[tag]/page/[page].js`

- Modify: `pages/category/[category]/page/[page].js`
- Modify: `pages/search/[keyword]/index.js`
- Modify: `pages/search/[keyword]/page/[page].js`

**Interfaces:**
- Consumes: `getStaticPathsBase({ filterFn, mapPageToParams, from, pageId, locale })` callers whose filters already select route-valid published content.
- Produces: all matching build-time paths and `fallback: 'blocking'` in normal builds; export mode remains all paths with `fallback: false`.

- [ ] **Step 1: Replace the priority-only test with an all-path test**

Remove `getPriorityPages` from the prefetch mock/imports, and replace the ISR test in `__tests__/lib/staticPaths.test.js` with:

```js
it('returns every matching path with blocking fallback in ISR mode', async () => {
  isExport.mockReturnValue(false)
  fetchGlobalAllData.mockResolvedValue({
    allPages: [
      { id: '1', slug: 'about', type: 'Page', status: 'Published' },
      { id: '2', slug: 'article/hello', type: 'Post', status: 'Published' },
      { id: '3', slug: 'article/world', type: 'Post', status: 'Published' }
    ]
  })

  await jest.isolateModulesAsync(async () => {
    const { getStaticPathsBase } = require('@/lib/build/staticPaths')
    const result = await getStaticPathsBase({
      filterFn: page => page.slug.startsWith('article/'),
      mapPageToParams: page => {
        const [prefix, slug] = page.slug.split('/')
        return { params: { prefix, slug } }
      }
    })

    expect(prefetchAllBlockMaps).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
    expect(result).toEqual({
      paths: [
        { params: { prefix: 'article', slug: 'hello' } },
        { params: { prefix: 'article', slug: 'world' } }
      ],
      fallback: 'blocking'
    })
  })
})
```

```js
    const source = read(file)
    expect(source).toMatch(/fallback:\s*['"]blocking['"]/)
    expect(source).not.toMatch(/fallback:\s*true/)
  })
})
```

### Task 3: Define the EdgeOne Locale Page-Data Contract Before Removing SSR

**Files:**
- Create: `scripts/verify-edgeone-build-contract.js`
- Create: `__tests__/scripts/verify-edgeone-build-contract.test.js`
- Modify: `edgeone.json`
- Modify: `package.json`

**Interfaces:**
- Consumes: `.next/BUILD_ID`, `.next/prerender-manifest.json`, and `edgeone.json`.
- Produces: `verifyBuildContract({ buildId, manifest, edgeoneConfig, locale }): { checkedRoutes: string[], buildId: string }` and CLI command `pnpm verify:edgeone-build`.

- [ ] **Step 1: Write failing verifier tests**

```js
const {
  REQUIRED_LOCALE_REWRITES,
  verifyBuildContract
} = require('../../scripts/verify-edgeone-build-contract')

describe('EdgeOne locale page-data contract', () => {
  const buildId = 'build-123'
  const manifest = {
    routes: {
      '/': {
        dataRoute: '/_next/data/build-123/index.json',
        initialRevalidateSeconds: 300
      },
      '/archive': {
        dataRoute: '/_next/data/build-123/archive.json',
        initialRevalidateSeconds: 300
      },
      '/page/2': {
        dataRoute: '/_next/data/build-123/page/2.json',
        initialRevalidateSeconds: 300
      }
    },
    dynamicRoutes: {}
  }

  test('accepts exact zh-CN static-data rewrites and 300-second routes', () => {
    expect(
      verifyBuildContract({
        buildId,
        manifest,
        edgeoneConfig: { rewrites: REQUIRED_LOCALE_REWRITES },
        locale: 'zh-CN'
      })
    ).toEqual({ buildId, checkedRoutes: ['/', '/archive', '/page/2'] })
  })

  test('rejects a missing locale rewrite', () => {
    expect(() =>
      verifyBuildContract({
        buildId,
        manifest,
        edgeoneConfig: { rewrites: REQUIRED_LOCALE_REWRITES.slice(1) },
        locale: 'zh-CN'
      })
    ).toThrow('missing EdgeOne locale data rewrite')
  })

  test('rejects SSR or a conflicting revalidation value', () => {
    const broken = JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(manifest))
    delete broken.routes['/archive']
    expect(() =>
      verifyBuildContract({
        buildId,
        manifest: broken,
        edgeoneConfig: { rewrites: REQUIRED_LOCALE_REWRITES },
        locale: 'zh-CN'
      })
    ).toThrow('missing prerender route: /archive')
  })
})
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run the verifier test and verify RED**

Run: `pnpm test -- __tests__/scripts/verify-edgeone-build-contract.test.js --runInBand`

Expected: FAIL with `Cannot find module '../../scripts/verify-edgeone-build-contract'`.

- [ ] **Step 3: Implement the manifest verifier**

Create `scripts/verify-edgeone-build-contract.js`:

```js
const fs = require('node:fs')
const path = require('node:path')

const REQUIRED_LOCALE_REWRITES = [
  {
    source: '/_next/data/:buildId/zh-CN.json',
    destination: '/_next/data/:buildId/index.json'
  },
  {
    source: '/_next/data/:buildId/zh-CN/archive.json',
    destination: '/_next/data/:buildId/archive.json'
  },
  {
    source: '/_next/data/:buildId/zh-CN/page/*.json',
    destination: '/_next/data/:buildId/page/:splat.json'
  }
]

function sameRule(left, right) {
  return left?.source === right.source && left?.destination === right.destination
}

function verifyBuildContract({ buildId, manifest, edgeoneConfig, locale }) {
  if (!buildId) throw new Error('missing Next.js Build ID')
  if (locale !== 'zh-CN') throw new Error(`unsupported contract locale: ${locale}`)

  for (const rule of REQUIRED_LOCALE_REWRITES) {
    if (!(edgeoneConfig.rewrites || []).some(candidate => sameRule(candidate, rule))) {
      throw new Error(`missing EdgeOne locale data rewrite: ${rule.source}`)
    }
  }

  const pagination = Object.keys(manifest.routes || {})
    .filter(route => /^\/page\/\d+$/.test(route))
    .sort((a, b) => Number(a.split('/').pop()) - Number(b.split('/').pop()))
  const checkedRoutes = ['/', '/archive', ...pagination]

  for (const route of checkedRoutes) {
    const entry = manifest.routes?.[route]
    if (!entry) throw new Error(`missing prerender route: ${route}`)
    if (entry.initialRevalidateSeconds !== 300) {
      throw new Error(`route ${route} revalidates at ${entry.initialRevalidateSeconds}`)
    }
    if (!entry.dataRoute?.startsWith(`/_next/data/${buildId}/`)) {
      throw new Error(`route ${route} has invalid data route: ${entry.dataRoute}`)
    }
  }

  return { buildId, checkedRoutes }
}

function verifyFromDisk(root = process.cwd()) {
  const buildId = fs.readFileSync(path.join(root, '.next/BUILD_ID'), 'utf8').trim()
  const manifest = JSON.parse(
    fs.readFileSync(path.join(root, '.next/prerender-manifest.json'), 'utf8')
  )
  const edgeoneConfig = JSON.parse(
    fs.readFileSync(path.join(root, 'edgeone.json'), 'utf8')
  )
  return verifyBuildContract({ buildId, manifest, edgeoneConfig, locale: 'zh-CN' })
}

if (require.main === module) {
  try {
    const result = verifyFromDisk()
    console.log(JSON.stringify(result, null, 2))
  } catch (error) {
    console.error(error.message)
    process.exitCode = 1
  }
}

module.exports = {
  REQUIRED_LOCALE_REWRITES,
  verifyBuildContract,
  verifyFromDisk
}
```

Add the three `REQUIRED_LOCALE_REWRITES` objects to a new top-level `rewrites` array in `edgeone.json`. Do not add a wildcard cache or header rule.

Add this script to `package.json`:

```json
"verify:edgeone-build": "node scripts/verify-edgeone-build-contract.js"
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Run unit tests and verify GREEN**

Run: `pnpm test -- __tests__/scripts/verify-edgeone-build-contract.test.js --runInBand`

Expected: PASS, three tests green.

```bash
pnpm build
pnpm verify:edgeone-build
```

- [ ] **Step 6: Commit the contract and narrow rewrites**

### Task 4: Convert Home, Archive, and General Pagination from SSR to ISR

**Files:**
- Modify: `pages/index.js`
- Modify: `pages/archive/index.js`
- Modify: `pages/page/[page].js`
- Modify: `__tests__/pages/locale-routing.test.js`
- Modify: `__tests__/pages/public-page-cache.test.js`

**Interfaces:**
- Consumes: `getPublicContentRevalidateSeconds(notionConfig)` from Task 1.
- Produces: `getStaticProps` for all three routes and `getStaticPaths` with blocking fallback for `/page/[page]`.

- [ ] **Step 1: Replace the former SSR regression test with the SSG contract**

Replace `__tests__/pages/locale-routing.test.js` with:

```js
const fs = require('fs')
const path = require('path')
const read = file => fs.readFileSync(path.resolve(process.cwd(), file), 'utf8')

const localePublicPages = [
  'pages/index.js',
  'pages/archive/index.js',
  'pages/page/[page].js'
]

describe('locale-prefixed public pages use build-time ISR', () => {
  test.each(localePublicPages)('%s uses static props and shared ISR', file => {
    const source = read(file)
    expect(source).toMatch(/export\s+async\s+function\s+getStaticProps\s*\(/)
    expect(source).not.toMatch(/getServerSideProps/)
    expect(source).toMatch(/getPublicContentRevalidateSeconds/)
  })

  test('general pagination pre-generates known pages and blocks on new ones', () => {
    const source = read('pages/page/[page].js')
    expect(source).toMatch(/export\s+async\s+function\s+getStaticPaths\s*\(/)
    expect(source).toMatch(/fallback:\s*['"]blocking['"]/)
  })
})
```

```js
const fs = require('fs')
const path = require('path')

describe('private routes stay outside public ISR', () => {
  test('auth remains server-side and never imports the public ISR policy', () => {
    const source = read('pages/auth/index.js')
    expect(source).toMatch(/getServerSideProps/)
    expect(source).not.toMatch(/publicContentCache/)
    expect(source).not.toMatch(/setPublicPageCache/)

  test.each([
    'pages/api/notion-comments.js',
    'pages/api/subscribe.js'
  ])('%s never imports the public ISR policy', file => {
    expect(read(file)).not.toMatch(/publicContentCache/)
  })
})
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Restore static pagination with input validation**

In `pages/page/[page].js`, import the Task 1 helper instead of `setPublicPageCache`, rename the data function to `getStaticProps({ params, locale })`, preserve all existing `notFound` checks, and return:

```js
return {
  props,
  revalidate: getPublicContentRevalidateSeconds(props.NOTION_CONFIG)
}
```

Add this exact static-path function before the default export:

```js
export async function getStaticPaths() {
  const { allPages = [], NOTION_CONFIG } = await fetchGlobalAllData({
    from: 'page-static-path'
  })
  const publishedPosts = allPages.filter(
    page => page.type === 'Post' && page.status === 'Published'
  )
  const postsPerPage = siteConfig('POSTS_PER_PAGE', 12, NOTION_CONFIG)
  const totalPages = Math.ceil(publishedPosts.length / postsPerPage)
  const paths = []

  for (let page = 2; page <= totalPages; page++) {
    paths.push({ params: { page: String(page) } })
  }

  return { paths, fallback: 'blocking' }
}
```

```js
const allPublicIsrPages = [
  'pages/index.js',
  'pages/archive/index.js',
  'pages/page/[page].js',
  'pages/[prefix]/index.js',
  'pages/[prefix]/[slug]/index.js',
  'pages/[prefix]/[slug]/[...suffix].js',
  'pages/tag/index.js',
  'pages/tag/[tag]/index.js',
  'pages/tag/[tag]/page/[page].js',
  'pages/category/index.js',
  'pages/category/[category]/index.js',
  'pages/category/[category]/page/[page].js',
  'pages/search/index.js',
  'pages/search/[keyword]/index.js',
  'pages/search/[keyword]/page/[page].js'
]

    expect(source).toMatch(
      /import\s+\{\s*getPublicContentRevalidateSeconds\s*\}\s+from\s+['"]@\/lib\/cache\/publicContentCache['"]/

      /revalidate:\s*getPublicContentRevalidateSeconds\(props\.NOTION_CONFIG\)/
    )
    expect(source).not.toMatch(/siteConfig\(\s*['"]NEXT_REVALIDATE_SECOND['"]/)
  })
})
```

### Task 6: Add Bounded Client Recovery for Next Page Data

**Files:**
- Create: `lib/client/nextDataRecovery.js`
- Create: `__tests__/lib/client/nextDataRecovery.test.js`
- Modify: `pages/_app.js`

**Interfaces:**
- Produces: `createNextDataFetch(options)`, `dataUrlToPageUrl(url)`, and `installNextDataRecovery({ windowObject, router })`.
- Scope: same-origin GET `/_next/data/*` only; two retry delays `[300, 1000]`; hard navigation guarded by `sessionStorage`.

- [ ] **Step 1: Write failing behavior tests**

Create tests covering these exact cases:

```js
import {
  createNextDataFetch,
  dataUrlToPageUrl
} from '@/lib/client/nextDataRecovery'

const response = status => ({ ok: status >= 200 && status < 300, status })

describe('Next page-data recovery', () => {
  test('maps locale page data back to the browser route', () => {
    expect(
      dataUrlToPageUrl(
        new URL('https://www.one2agi.com/_next/data/b1/zh-CN/article/3213.json?theme=next')
      )
    ).toBe('/zh-CN/article/3213?theme=next')
    expect(
      dataUrlToPageUrl(
        new URL('https://www.one2agi.com/_next/data/b1/index.json')
      )
    ).toBe('/')
  })

  test.each([545, 502, 503, 504])('retries status %s twice', async status => {
    const originalFetch = jest
      .fn()
      .mockResolvedValueOnce(response(status))
      .mockResolvedValueOnce(response(status))
      .mockResolvedValueOnce(response(200))
    const wait = jest.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined)
    const recoveredFetch = createNextDataFetch({
      originalFetch,
      origin: 'https://www.one2agi.com',
      buildId: 'b1',
      storage: { getItem: jest.fn(), setItem: jest.fn() },
      hardNavigate: jest.fn(),
      wait
    })

    await expect(
      recoveredFetch('/_next/data/b1/zh-CN/archive.json')
    ).resolves.toEqual(response(200))
    expect(originalFetch).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(3)
    expect(wait.mock.calls.map(([delay]) => delay)).toEqual([300, 1000])
  })

  test('retries a disconnect but never an AbortError', async () => {
    const wait = jest.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined)
    const disconnectFetch = jest
      .fn()
      .mockRejectedValueOnce(new TypeError('fetch failed'))
      .mockResolvedValue(response(200))
    const recovered = createNextDataFetch({
      originalFetch: disconnectFetch,
      origin: 'https://www.one2agi.com',
      buildId: 'b1',
      storage: { getItem: jest.fn(), setItem: jest.fn() },
      hardNavigate: jest.fn(),
      wait
    })
    await recovered('/_next/data/b1/zh-CN.json')
    expect(disconnectFetch).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(2)

    const abort = Object.assign(new Error('cancelled'), { name: 'AbortError' })
    const abortFetch = jest.fn().mockRejectedValue(abort)
    const abortRecovered = createNextDataFetch({
      originalFetch: abortFetch,
      origin: 'https://www.one2agi.com',
      buildId: 'b1',
      storage: { getItem: jest.fn(), setItem: jest.fn() },
      hardNavigate: jest.fn(),
      wait
    })
    await expect(abortRecovered('/_next/data/b1/zh-CN.json')).rejects.toBe(abort)
    expect(abortFetch).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1)
  })

  test.each([
    ['https://api.example.com/_next/data/b1/x.json', { method: 'GET' }],
    ['/api/payment/create', { method: 'POST' }],
    ['/_next/data/b1/x.json', { method: 'POST' }]
  ])('does not retry ineligible request %s', async (url, init) => {
    const originalFetch = jest.fn().mockResolvedValue(response(545))
    const recovered = createNextDataFetch({
      originalFetch,
      origin: 'https://www.one2agi.com',
      buildId: 'b1',
      storage: { getItem: jest.fn(), setItem: jest.fn() },
      hardNavigate: jest.fn(),
      wait: jest.fn()
    })
    await recovered(url, init)
    expect(originalFetch).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1)
  })

  test('hard-navigates only once after retries are exhausted', async () => {
    const storageValues = new Map()
    const storage = {
      getItem: key => storageValues.get(key),
      setItem: (key, value) => storageValues.set(key, value)
    }
    const hardNavigate = jest.fn()
    const originalFetch = jest.fn().mockResolvedValue(response(545))
    const recovered = createNextDataFetch({
      originalFetch,
      origin: 'https://www.one2agi.com',
      buildId: 'b1',
      storage,
      hardNavigate,
      wait: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined)
    })

    await recovered('/_next/data/b1/zh-CN/archive.json')
    await recovered('/_next/data/b1/zh-CN/archive.json')
    expect(hardNavigate).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1)
    expect(hardNavigate).toHaveBeenCalledWith('/zh-CN/archive')
  })
})
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run tests and verify RED**

Run: `pnpm test -- __tests__/lib/client/nextDataRecovery.test.js --runInBand`

Expected: FAIL because the module does not exist.

- [ ] **Step 3: Implement the isolated recovery module**

Create `lib/client/nextDataRecovery.js` with these constants and control flow:

```js
const RETRYABLE_STATUSES = new Set([545, 502, 503, 504])
const RETRY_DELAYS_MS = [300, 1000]
const GUARD_PREFIX = 'notion-next:data-recovery:'

const sleep = delay => new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, delay))

function requestMethod(input, init) {
  return String(init?.method || input?.method || 'GET').toUpperCase()
}

function requestUrl(input, origin) {
  const value = typeof input === 'string' || input instanceof URL ? input : input.url
  return new URL(value, origin)
}

function isEligible(input, init, origin) {
  const url = requestUrl(input, origin)
  return (
    requestMethod(input, init) === 'GET' &&
    url.origin === origin &&
    url.pathname.startsWith('/_next/data/')
  )
}

export function dataUrlToPageUrl(url) {
  const parts = url.pathname.split('/').filter(Boolean)
  const routeParts = parts.slice(3)
  const last = routeParts.length - 1
  routeParts[last] = routeParts[last].replace(/\.json$/, '')
  if (routeParts[last] === 'index') routeParts.pop()
  const pathname = `/${routeParts.join('/')}` || '/'
  return `${pathname}${url.search}`
}

function recoverOnce({ url, buildId, storage, hardNavigate }) {
  const pageUrl = dataUrlToPageUrl(url)
  const guardKey = `${GUARD_PREFIX}${buildId}:${pageUrl}`
  if (storage?.getItem(guardKey)) return
  storage?.setItem(guardKey, '1')
  hardNavigate(pageUrl)
}

export function createNextDataFetch({
  originalFetch,
  origin,
  buildId,
  storage,
  hardNavigate,
  wait = sleep
}) {
  return async function nextDataFetch(input, init) {
    if (!isEligible(input, init, origin)) return originalFetch(input, init)

    const url = requestUrl(input, origin)
    for (let attempt = 0; attempt <= RETRY_DELAYS_MS.length; attempt++) {
      try {
        const result = await originalFetch(input, init)
        if (!RETRYABLE_STATUSES.has(result.status)) return result
        if (attempt === RETRY_DELAYS_MS.length) {
          recoverOnce({ url, buildId, storage, hardNavigate })
          return result
        }
      } catch (error) {
        if (error?.name === 'AbortError') throw error
        if (attempt === RETRY_DELAYS_MS.length) {
          recoverOnce({ url, buildId, storage, hardNavigate })
          throw error
        }
      }
      await wait(RETRY_DELAYS_MS[attempt])
    }
  }
}

function clearBuildGuards(storage, buildId) {
  const prefix = `${GUARD_PREFIX}${buildId}:`
  for (let index = storage.length - 1; index >= 0; index--) {
    const key = storage.key(index)
    if (key?.startsWith(prefix)) storage.removeItem(key)
  }
}

export function installNextDataRecovery({ windowObject, router }) {
  const originalWindowFetch = windowObject.fetch
  const originalFetch = (...args) => originalWindowFetch.apply(windowObject, args)
  const buildId = windowObject.__NEXT_DATA__?.buildId || 'unknown-build'
  const wrapped = createNextDataFetch({
    originalFetch,
    origin: windowObject.location.origin,
    buildId,
    storage: windowObject.sessionStorage,
    hardNavigate: url => windowObject.location.assign(url)
  })
  const clear = () => clearBuildGuards(windowObject.sessionStorage, buildId)

  windowObject.fetch = wrapped
  router.events.on('routeChangeComplete', clear)

  return () => {
    router.events.off('routeChangeComplete', clear)
    if (windowObject.fetch === wrapped) windowObject.fetch = originalWindowFetch
  }
}
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Install it once in the application shell**

Add imports to `pages/_app.js`:

```js

```

Inside `MyApp`, immediately after `const route = useRouter()`, add:

```js

  return installNextDataRecovery({ windowObject: window, router: route })
}, [route])
```

### Task 8: Add Reproducible Build and Production Probes

**Files:**
- Create: `scripts/probe-edgeone-stability.mjs`
- Create: `__tests__/scripts/probe-edgeone-stability.test.js`
- Modify: `package.json`
- Modify: `.gitignore`

**Interfaces:**
- Consumes: `--base-url`, `--mode=warm|cold`, `--count`, and optional `--out`.
- Produces: JSON report with Build ID, request records, status counts, network-error count, cache headers, request IDs, and P95.

- [ ] **Step 1: Write failing probe helper tests**

```js
describe('EdgeOne probe helpers', () => {
  test('uses canonical sitemap URLs without cache-busting queries', async () => {
    const { extractCanonicalUrls } = await import('../../scripts/probe-edgeone-stability.mjs')
    const urls = extractCanonicalUrls(
      '<loc>https://www.one2agi.com/</loc><loc>https://www.one2agi.com/article/3213</loc>',
      new URL('https://www.one2agi.com')
    )
    expect(urls).toEqual([
      'https://www.one2agi.com/',
      'https://www.one2agi.com/article/3213'
    ])
    expect(urls.join(' ')).not.toMatch(/[?&](_|cb|cacheBust)=/)
  })

  test('counts 545 and network disconnects separately and computes P95', async () => {
    const { summarize } = await import('../../scripts/probe-edgeone-stability.mjs')
    const summary = summarize([
      { status: 200, durationMs: 100 },
      { status: 545, durationMs: 300 },
      { networkError: 'fetch failed', durationMs: 200 }
    ])
    expect(summary.statuses).toEqual({ 200: 1, 545: 1 })
    expect(summary.networkErrors).toBe(1)
    expect(summary.p95Ms).toBe(300)
  })
})
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run the test and verify RED**

Run: `pnpm test -- __tests__/scripts/probe-edgeone-stability.test.js --runInBand`

Expected: FAIL because the probe module does not exist.

- [ ] **Step 3: Implement canonical URL collection and summaries**

The probe module must export these complete helpers:

```js
export function extractCanonicalUrls(xml, baseUrl) {
  const found = [...xml.matchAll(/<loc>([^<]+)<\/loc>/g)]
    .map(match => new URL(match[1].trim(), baseUrl))
    .filter(url => url.origin === baseUrl.origin)
    .map(url => {
      url.hash = ''
      return url.href
    })
  return [...new Set(found)]
}

export function summarize(records) {
  const statuses = {}
  const durations = records.map(record => record.durationMs).sort((a, b) => a - b)
  for (const record of records) {
    if (record.status) statuses[record.status] = (statuses[record.status] || 0) + 1
  }
  const p95Index = Math.max(0, Math.ceil(durations.length * 0.95) - 1)
  return {
    requests: records.length,
    statuses,
    networkErrors: records.filter(record => record.networkError).length,
    p95Ms: durations[p95Index] || 0
  }
}
```

The CLI portion must:

1. Parse `--base-url=https://www.one2agi.com`, `--mode=warm|cold`, `--count=180`, and `--out=<path>`.
2. Fetch `/` and extract `buildId` from `__NEXT_DATA__`.
3. Read `locale` from `__NEXT_DATA__`; require `zh-CN` for this production contract.
4. Fetch `/sitemap.xml`, retain only same-origin canonical URLs, and add `/archive`.
5. Add each route's `/_next/data/{buildId}/{locale}/...json` URL. The localized home data URL is `/_next/data/{buildId}/{locale}.json`; `/archive` becomes `/_next/data/{buildId}/{locale}/archive.json`.
6. Build exactly `count` requests by round-robin over canonical HTML, page data, and one hashed `/_next/static` asset discovered in the home HTML.
7. For `warm`, send ordinary GETs. For `cold`, send `Cache-Control: no-cache` and `Pragma: no-cache` headers against the same canonical URLs; never append random query parameters.
8. Use concurrency 6 for warm and 3 for cold.
9. Record `status`, `durationMs`, `x-cache`, `eo-cache-status`, `x-nws-log-uuid`, `x-edgeone-request-id`, `x-function-request-id`, and any fetch exception string.
10. Write `{ generatedAt, baseUrl, buildId, mode, summary, records }` as formatted JSON.
11. Exit non-zero when `statuses['545'] > 0` or `networkErrors > 0`.

Use this exact request record body:

```js
async function probeOne(url, mode) {
  const started = performance.now()
  try {
    const response = await fetch(url, {
      redirect: 'follow',
      headers:
        mode === 'cold'
          ? { 'cache-control': 'no-cache', pragma: 'no-cache' }
          : undefined
    })
    await response.arrayBuffer()
    return {
      url,
      status: response.status,
      durationMs: Math.round(performance.now() - started),
      cacheStatus:
        response.headers.get('eo-cache-status') ||
        response.headers.get('x-cache'),
      nwsLogUuid: response.headers.get('x-nws-log-uuid'),
      edgeoneRequestId: response.headers.get('x-edgeone-request-id'),
      functionRequestId: response.headers.get('x-function-request-id')
    }
  } catch (error) {
    return {
      url,
      durationMs: Math.round(performance.now() - started),
      networkError: error?.cause?.code || error.message
    }
  }
}
```

Add to `package.json`:

```json
"probe:edgeone": "node scripts/probe-edgeone-stability.mjs"
```

Add this exact entry to `.gitignore`:

```gitignore
/.artifacts/
```

Run:

```bash

pnpm probe:edgeone -- --base-url=https://www.one2agi.com --mode=warm --count=5 --out=.artifacts/edgeone-probe-dry-run.json
```

Expected: tests PASS; report has five records and a Build ID. `.artifacts/` must remain ignored and uncommitted.


### Task 9: Document, Build, and Verify the Complete Local Product

**Files:**
- Modify: `docs/user-guide/config/site-basics.md`
- Modify: `docs/user-guide/reference/features.md`
- Modify: `docs/user-guide/deploy/edgeone-pages.md`

**Interfaces:**
- Consumes: all prior tasks.
- Produces: a locally releasable branch with recorded build, browser, function, and package-size evidence.

- [ ] **Step 1: Update user-facing behavior documentation**

Document these exact product statements:

```markdown
- Public pages are generated ahead of time and become eligible for refresh after 300 seconds.
- 300 seconds is not a timer: the first request after expiry triggers regeneration.
- A Notion edit does not require deployment; a newly published route may be slower on its first request.
- Comments, payments, subscriptions, article passwords, and APIs remain dynamic.
- The knowledge graph retains its independent 10-minute refresh policy.
- EdgeOne locale page-data rewrites are deliberately limited to `/_next/data/*`; never broaden them to `/api/*`.
```

Remove old examples that recommend 5 or 60 seconds as this deployment's default. Do not claim exact five-minute visibility or global pre-warming.

```bash
BUILD_ID=$(cat .next/BUILD_ID)
curl -fsS -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}\n' http://127.0.0.1:8088/zh-CN/
curl -fsS -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}\n' http://127.0.0.1:8088/zh-CN/archive
curl -fsS -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}\n' "http://127.0.0.1:8088/_next/data/$BUILD_ID/zh-CN.json"
curl -fsS -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}\n' "http://127.0.0.1:8088/_next/data/$BUILD_ID/zh-CN/archive.json"
curl -fsS -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}\n' http://127.0.0.1:8088/api/knowledge-graph
KNOWLEDGE_GRAPH_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8088/api/knowledge-graph pnpm smoke:knowledge-graph
```

Expected: HTML and page-data requests return 200. The graph may return its valid initialization 202 once; the smoke tool must accept only that exact contract or a valid 200 graph. Also request one actual article HTML/data pair and `/api/notion-comments` GET; do not POST comments or call payment creation.

For representative public HTML and page-data responses, also inspect headers and require the application-controlled cache policy to include `s-maxage=300` and `stale-while-revalidate=86400`. If the EdgeOne adapter rewrites these headers, record both the origin/inner-function header and the final CDN header instead of assuming they are equivalent.

- [ ] **Step 5: Perform browser navigation verification**

Open the local EdgeOne URL, navigate home -> tag -> article -> archive -> pagination without refresh, and inspect the browser console/network panel.

Expected: no locale `/_next/data` 404, no `Loading initial props cancelled` caused by a failed request, no reload loop, and no new Live2D error attributable to route recovery. Use a temporary local response override or the recovery unit tests for synthetic 545; do not add a production failure injector.

- [ ] **Step 6: Commit documentation after all local gates pass**

```bash
git add docs/user-guide/config/site-basics.md docs/user-guide/reference/features.md docs/user-guide/deploy/edgeone-pages.md
git commit -m "docs(edgeone): explain five-minute ISR behavior"
```

- [ ] **Step 7: Review the exact branch diff**

Run:

```bash
git status --short
git diff --check main...HEAD
git diff --stat main...HEAD
git log --oneline main..HEAD
```

Expected: only planned files are committed; `.serena/project.yml` and `AGENTS.md` remain modified but unstaged; no whitespace errors; every commit is independently revertible.

---

## Deployment and Production Acceptance Gate

This is a release gate, not permission to mutate production content.

- [ ] Merge the reviewed feature branch into `main` without staging `.serena/project.yml` or `AGENTS.md`.
- [ ] Push `main`; EdgeOne deploys automatically.
- [ ] Poll the canonical home page until its Build ID differs from the pre-deployment Build ID.
- [ ] Run the warm acceptance probe:

```bash
pnpm probe:edgeone -- --base-url=https://www.one2agi.com --mode=warm --count=180 --out=.artifacts/edgeone-warm-180.json
```

- [ ] Run the deliberate no-cache acceptance probe at concurrency 3:

```bash
pnpm probe:edgeone -- --base-url=https://www.one2agi.com --mode=cold --count=180 --out=.artifacts/edgeone-cold-180.json
```

- [ ] Require both reports to show `545: 0` and `networkErrors: 0`; cached public-page P95 target is below 2000 ms.
- [ ] Confirm the cold report's EdgeOne cache-status headers actually show bypass/miss/revalidation behavior. If EdgeOne ignores request `no-cache`, label the run as canonical repeated traffic rather than falsely claiming 180 cache misses, and use EdgeOne's documented read-only cache-bypass mechanism before acceptance.
- [ ] Read-only smoke: home, archive, tag, category, search, representative article, article page data, comment GET, payment endpoint health without creating an order, password page, and knowledge graph.
- [ ] If the user performs or separately authorizes a Notion edit, verify existing content changes after the 300-second eligibility interval plus a triggering request.
- [ ] If the user performs or separately authorizes a new publication, verify list discovery, first blocking article generation, and second cached delivery.
- [ ] Do not publish, unpublish, post a comment, or create a real order without separate authorization.
- [ ] If locale navigation regresses, revert only `feat(pages): move public listings to ISR` and the locale routing contract commit. If browser recovery regresses, revert only `fix(router): recover transient page-data failures`. Keep the prior Clerk/middleware removal intact.

## Completion Evidence

Do not claim the incident resolved from unit tests alone. Completion requires:

- full Jest, lint, type-check, and production build output;
- the passing prerender/locale contract report;
- EdgeOne CLI HTML, page-data, comment-read, and graph responses;
- edge package size evidence below 5 MB;
- a changed production Build ID;
- both 180-request production reports with zero 545 and zero empty/network replies;
- explicit disclosure if the optional Notion mutation checks were not authorized and therefore remain untested.
